objective: Implement the P0 GAP-002 closeout-verifier slice so required CI conclusions that are not explicit success, including neutral and skipped, cannot satisfy PR closeout claims such as tests_passed or ci_green.
ecosystemReviewFindings: Claim-vs-evidence must be canonical closeout: done, green, merge-ready, review-addressed, root-tidy, and goal-complete claims must be backed by current, explicit evidence.

Runtime proof passed requires runtime and external evidence freshness; degraded or non-success evidence should produce blocked_runtime, blocked_validation, fail, or unknown instead of green-looking pass.
Harness must stay a thin verifier cockpit over Codex-native and external runtime facts rather than blending CI, review, Linear, local validation, and final prose into one readiness truth.
deepModuleBoundary: PR closeout required-check conclusion classification, claim-builder required-check input scoping, and focused tests only. This slice hardens existing pr-closeout/v1 claims; it does not introduce review-state, external-state, delivery-truth, runtime-card, or GitHub API fetching behavior.
implementationThesis: Closeout evidence should treat only explicit successful required-check conclusions as passing. Neutral, skipped, empty, cancelled, timed-out, pending, missing, stale, or unknown required-check evidence may still orient next action, but it cannot support passing tests_passed or ci_green claims. Optional skipped or neutral checks should remain diagnostic and must not create a new false blocker when all required checks pass.

currentEvidence.observed: isPassingCheck currently returns true for SUCCESS, PASSED, PASS, NEUTRAL, and SKIPPED.
currentEvidence.gap: NEUTRAL and SKIPPED are pass-looking in PR closeout summaries and in ci_green/tests_passed claim builders.

currentEvidence.observed: tests_passed and ci_green use isPassingCheck to determine pass status.
currentEvidence.gap: A skipped or neutral required check can currently make allChecksPassing true when the evidence is otherwise current.

currentEvidence.observed: Existing tests cover missing, stale, pending, failed, and successful checks, but do not pin neutral/skipped as non-pass closeout evidence.
currentEvidence.gap: The GAP-002 regression has no focused table-driven proof.

currentEvidence.observed: GAP-002 identifies skipped or neutral required CI credited as passing closeout evidence.
currentEvidence.gap: The audit expectation is not yet enforced by production code or tests.

outOfScope: changing GitHub, CircleCI, CodeRabbit, or Linear API clients
changing delivery-truth or review-state packet schemas
changing status names or public pr-closeout schema versions unless tests prove a schema-compatible blocker requires it
external writes to GitHub, Linear, CI, or review services during this local implementation slice
acceptanceCriteria: isPassingCheck returns true only for explicit success-style statuses: SUCCESS, PASSED, and PASS.
Required checks with NEUTRAL or SKIPPED conclusions do not produce pass for tests_passed or ci_green.
Optional checks with NEUTRAL or SKIPPED conclusions do not block tests_passed or ci_green when all required checks pass.
Neutral and skipped required-check evidence is represented as blocked or unknown closeout state, not ready.
Report check summaries count neutral and skipped required checks as unknown rather than passed.
Existing successful, failed, pending, stale, and missing check behavior remains covered.

Focused tests cover success, required skipped, required neutral, optional skipped, optional neutral, cancelled, missing, stale, and timed-out closeout states or explicitly rely on existing tests for already-covered states.
Goal receipt records Project Brain inputs, GAP-002, ecosystem review alignment, validation commands, and any blocker.

automationPlan.name: intent artifact JSON validation
proves: slice intent is syntactically valid before review

automationPlan.name: focused PR closeout regression
automationPlan.command: pnpm vitest run src/lib/pr-closeout.test.ts -t "required CI conclusions"
proves: neutral and skipped required checks cannot satisfy closeout pass claims, and optional neutral or skipped checks do not create false blockers when required checks pass

automationPlan.name: broader PR closeout regression
proves: existing PR closeout behavior remains compatible

automationPlan.name: goal board validation
proves: goal state and receipts remain valid after slice updates

reviewQuestions: Does this intent close GAP-002 without over-expanding into external-state or delivery-truth rewrites?

Are neutral and skipped required checks correctly treated as non-pass evidence without making optional skipped/neutral checks into false blockers or making successful, failed, pending, missing, or stale behavior ambiguous?
Are the validation gates sufficient to prove the claim-vs-evidence false-success regression?

reviewFindingsAdopted.finding: Success-only classifier hardening can falsely block closeout when optional skipped or neutral checks remain in universal claim-builder inputs.
reviewFindingsAdopted.resolution: Expanded scope to include claim-builder required-check input scoping and optional skipped/neutral regression fixtures.

reviewFindingsAdopted.finding: The intent referenced src/lib/pr-closeout/claim-builders.ts as a regression locus while excluding it from inScope.
reviewFindingsAdopted.resolution: Added src/lib/pr-closeout/claim-builders.ts to inScope and guardedPathGlobs.

postReviewMutationPolicy: any material mutation to objective, inScope, outOfScope, acceptanceCriteria, automationPlan, reviewPlan, stopConditions, or rollback resets reviewStatus to pending
linearMutationPlan: No external Linear mutation during this local implementation slice. JSC-363 remains linked in the intent and receipt; Linear writes are already classified as blocked by timeout in R055/R056.
stopConditions: Stop if the required behavior needs a public schema/status change rather than a narrow classifier and required-check input-scope fix.
Stop if tests reveal that a provider uses NEUTRAL or SKIPPED as the only success signal for an explicitly required check and the repo lacks a modeled exception policy.
Stop if focused validation cannot execute the changed production classifier path.
Stop if implementation would touch runtime-card, review-state, external-state, delivery-truth, GitHub API, Linear API, or broad PR closeout schema surfaces.
rollback: Revert pr-closeout classifier/test edits if they break a documented success-only provider contract, then replace the approach with an explicit exception model reviewed by Judge/PM.
Keep the regression that prevents NEUTRAL and SKIPPED from being counted as generic passing closeout evidence unless a future exception contract is explicitly implemented.
Do not roll back unrelated dirty worktree changes.
assumptions: GitHub/CircleCI/CodeRabbit required-check closeout proof should require explicit success-style conclusions.
Neutral and skipped can orient next action but are not claim-supporting pass evidence.
JSC-363 remains the Linear owner for this lifecycle goal even though external tracker mutation is currently blocked.
The existing pr-closeout/v1 claim status vocabulary can express the fix through blocked, fail, unknown, freshness, blockerClass, and blockers without a schema migration.
